title: Hard on yourself, gentle with everyone else
caption:
You've got it backwards.

You're soft on your own sin and sharp with everyone around you. You call your porn a "struggle" and your laziness a "season" — grace you'd never extend to another man. Then you turn around and crucify her over one short text, snap at your mom, run hardest on the people who can least defend themselves.

That's not humility and gentleness. It's the exact opposite of both.

Look at how Jesus actually moved. He wept with the grieving. He ate with the men everyone wrote off. And then he braided a whip and cleared the temple. Gentle with the broken, ruthless with the rot. He never once confused a sinner with their sin.

"Neither do I condemn you. Go and sin no more." — John 8:11. Full grace for the person. No grace for the sin.

So flip it back. Be ruthless with the man in the mirror — kill your sin like it's trying to kill you, because it is. And be patient with the man across the table — cover his the way Christ covered yours.

Hard on your sin. Gentle with their souls.

—

Comment "mirror" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.

#ethos #christianmen #faithoverfear #godlymen #manofgod #disciplineequalsfreedom #masculinity #humility #christianity #scripture
theme: He's hard on himself, gentle with others.
